title: BIC Orange Fine Ballpoint Pens, Fine Point (0.8 mm), Red Ink, Box of 20 - Writing Pens with Long-Lasting Ink
features: This box of 20 red ballpoints is great for many sessions of taking notes and drawing
The ball bearing is engineered to perfection so the ink evenly flows from the point for seamlessly smooth writing
These BIC Cristal Orange Original Fine ballpoint pens are made to last so they're always ready when you need one
Long-lasting BIC Biro pens made from minimal raw materials and certified with the NF Environnement eco-label

A box of 20 red BIC Orange Original Fine ballpoint pens with barrels that were, are and will always be orange. This ballpoint pen was introduced in 1961 for people looking for a precision writing instrument and features a uniquely fine 0.8 mm point. Taking after its big brother, the BIC Cristal Original, this pen offers the same state-of-the-art technological trio: a point, a perfectly spherical ball, and high-quality ink manufactured in the BIC plants. This system ensures even ink flow for accurate fineline writing and intricate drawings. BIC has been tirelessly improving the BIC Orange Original Fine pen since it came out. It gradually got lighter and the instant-dry ink is constantly being perfected for writing with an ever-gentler touch. BIC even gave it a twin brother: the Cristal Original Fine ballpoint pen that has a translucent orange barrel instead of an opaque one. A mark of quality. BIC makes this pen at its own plants in Europe using unmatched expertise that has been passed down the generations. BIC has it down to a fine art.
description: From the manufacturer The BIC Orange ballpoint pens offers you quality and reliability with a design that is hard to lose track of, making them a good choice for a busy office environment. To save you time and to add a sophisticated touch to these essential and iconic pens, the cap and end plug match the colour of the ink so you can easily find the one you need. Each pen also offers strong writing performance with a 3,500 m writing length on average*. (*SGS test 2013/2014 - average writing length for blue and black cartridges.)
